message:
fix(termwiz/windows): error explicitly when native renderer asked for alt-screen (ft-uic0x)

WindowsTerminal::enter_alternate_screen / exit_alternate_screen used
to fall through to a silent Ok(()) for the WindowsConsoleRenderer
branch (the CreateConsoleScreenBuffer / SetConsoleActiveScreenBuffer
plumbing was a TODO). That let full-screen TUIs assume they captured
the screen, then corrupt main-buffer scrollback when they emitted
output expecting the alt buffer.

Per the bead's acceptance criterion, enter now `bail!`s loudly for
the native renderer, naming the bead and pointing at the Terminfo
renderer as the supported path. exit mirrors the contract for any
caller that managed to flip `in_alternate_screen=true` against this
renderer (unreachable through the public API once enter bails first;
defensive for direct field manipulation), but stays a quiet Ok when
`in_alternate_screen=false` so the standard Drop path doesn't spam a
warning log on every Windows-native session shutdown.

Verification: cargo check -p termwiz clean on aarch64-apple-darwin
(unix.rs is what builds here; windows.rs is the platform-gated
sibling). Native cross-target verification deferred to CI workers
with a Windows toolchain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
